6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([

8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)

28	export const rawConfigSchema = z

50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

12	import { THEMES, type Theme } from "@/registry/themes"

16	export { BASES, type Base }
17	export { STYLES, type Style }
18	export { THEMES, type Theme }

22	
23	export type BaseName = Base["name"]
24	export type StyleName = Style["name"]
25	export type ThemeName = Theme["name"]
26	export type BaseColorName = BaseColor["name"]
27	export type ChartColorName = Theme["name"]

29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const
35	
36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}
54	
55	export const MENU_ACCENTS = [
56	  { value: "subtle", label: "Subtle" },
57	  { value: "bold", label: "Bold" },
58	] as const
59	
60	export type MenuAccent = (typeof MENU_ACCENTS)[number]
61	export type MenuAccentValue = MenuAccent["value"]
62	
63	export const MENU_COLORS = [
64	  { value: "default", label: "Default" },
65	  { value: "inverted", label: "Inverted" },
66	  { value: "default-translucent", label: "Default Translucent" },
67	  { value: "inverted-translucent", label: "Inverted Translucent" },
68	] as const
69	
70	export type MenuColor = (typeof MENU_COLORS)[number]
71	
72	export type MenuColorValue = MenuColor["value"]
73	
74	export const RADII = [
75	  { name: "default", label: "Default", value: "" },
76	  { name: "none", label: "None", value: "0" },
77	  { name: "small", label: "Small", value: "0.45rem" },
78	  { name: "medium", label: "Medium", value: "0.625rem" },
79	  { name: "large", label: "Large", value: "0.875rem" },
80	] as const
81	
82	export type Radius = (typeof RADII)[number]
83	
84	export type RadiusValue = Radius["name"]

86	export const designSystemConfigSchema = z
87	  .object({
88	    base: z.enum(BASES.map((b) => b.name) as [BaseName, ...BaseName[]]),
89	    style: z.enum(STYLES.map((s) => s.name) as [StyleName, ...StyleName[]]),
90	    iconLibrary: z.enum(
91	      Object.keys(iconLibraries) as [IconLibraryName, ...IconLibraryName[]]
92	    ),
93	    baseColor: z
94	      .enum(
95	        BASE_COLORS.map((c) => c.name) as [BaseColorName, ...BaseColorName[]]
96	      )
97	      .default("neutral"),
98	    theme: z.enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ThemeName, ...ThemeName[]]),
99	    chartColor: z
100	      .enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ChartColorName, ...ChartColorName[]])
101	      .default("neutral"),
102	    font: z.enum(fontValues).default("inter"),
103	    fontHeading: z.enum(fontHeadingValues).default("inherit"),
104	    item: z.string().optional(),
105	    rtl: z.boolean().default(false),
106	    menuAccent: z
107	      .enum(
108	        MENU_ACCENTS.map((a) => a.value) as [
109	          MenuAccentValue,
110	          ...MenuAccentValue[],
111	        ]
112	      )
113	      .default("subtle"),
114	    menuColor: z
115	      .enum(
116	        MENU_COLORS.map((m) => m.value) as [MenuColorValue, ...MenuColorValue[]]
117	      )
118	      .default("default"),
119	    radius: z
120	      .enum(RADII.map((r) => r.name) as [RadiusValue, ...RadiusValue[]])
121	      .default("default"),
122	    template: z
123	      .enum([
124	        "next",
125	        "next-monorepo",
126	        "start",
127	        "react-router",
128	        "vite",
129	        "vite-monorepo",
130	        "react-router-monorepo",
131	        "start-monorepo",
132	        "astro",
133	        "astro-monorepo",
134	        "laravel",
135	      ])
136	      .default("next")
137	      .optional(),
138	  })
139	  .refine(
140	    (data) => {
141	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
142	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.theme)
143	    },
144	    (data) => ({
145	      message: `Theme "${data.theme}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
146	      path: ["theme"],
147	    })
148	  )
149	  .refine(
150	    (data) => {
151	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
152	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.chartColor)
153	    },
154	    (data) => ({
155	      message: `Chart color "${data.chartColor}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
156	      path: ["chartColor"],
157	    })
158	  )

407	export function getThemesForBaseColor(baseColorName: string) {
408	  const baseColorNames = BASE_COLORS.map((bc) => bc.name)
409	
410	  return THEMES.filter((theme) => {
411	    if (theme.name === baseColorName) {
412	      return true
413	    }
414	    return !baseColorNames.includes(theme.name)
415	  })
416	}
417	
418	export function getBase(name: BaseName) {
419	  return BASES.find((base) => base.name === name)
420	}
421	
422	export function getStyle(name: StyleName) {
423	  return STYLES.find((style) => style.name === name)
424	}
425	
426	export function getTheme(name: ThemeName) {
427	  return THEMES.find((theme) => theme.name === name)
428	}
429	
430	export function getBaseColor(name: BaseColorName) {
431	  return BASE_COLORS.find((color) => color.name === name)
432	}
433	
434	export function getIconLibrary(name: IconLibraryName) {
435	  return iconLibraries[name]
436	}
437	
438	// Builds a registry:theme item from a design system config.
439	export function buildRegistryTheme(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
440	  const baseColor = getBaseColor(config.baseColor)
441	  const theme = getTheme(config.theme)
442	
443	  if (!baseColor || !theme) {
444	    throw new Error(
445	      `Base color "${config.baseColor}" or theme "${config.theme}" not found`
446	    )
447	  }
448	
449	  // Merge base color and theme CSS vars.
450	  const lightVars: Record<string, string> = {
451	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
452	    ...(theme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
453	  }
454	  const darkVars: Record<string, string> = {
455	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
456	    ...(theme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
457	  }
458	  const themeVars: Record<string, string> = {}
459	
460	  // Apply chart color override.
461	  const chartTheme = getTheme(config.chartColor)
462	  if (chartTheme) {
463	    const chartLight = chartTheme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>
464	    const chartDark = chartTheme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>
465	    for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
466	      const key = `chart-${i}`
467	      if (chartLight?.[key]) lightVars[key] = chartLight[key]
468	      if (chartDark?.[key]) darkVars[key] = chartDark[key]
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  // Apply menu accent transformation.
473	  if (config.menuAccent === "bold") {
474	    lightVars.accent = lightVars.primary
475	    lightVars["accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
476	    darkVars.accent = darkVars.primary
477	    darkVars["accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
478	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent"] = lightVars.primary
479	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
480	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent"] = darkVars.primary
481	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
482	  }
483	
484	  // Apply radius transformation.
485	  if (config.radius && config.radius !== "default") {
486	    const radius = RADII.find((r) => r.name === config.radius)
487	    if (radius && radius.value) {
488	      lightVars.radius = radius.value
489	    }
490	  }
491	
492	  return {
493	    name: `${config.baseColor}-${config.theme}`,
494	    type: "registry:theme" as const,
495	    cssVars: {
496	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
497	      light: lightVars,
498	      dark: darkVars,
499	    },
500	  }
501	}
502	
503	// Builds a registry:base item from a design system config.
504	export function buildRegistryBase(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
505	  const baseItem = getBase(config.base)
506	  const iconLibraryItem = getIconLibrary(config.iconLibrary)
507	  const normalizedFontHeading =
508	    config.fontHeading === config.font ? "inherit" : config.fontHeading
509	
510	  if (!baseItem || !iconLibraryItem) {
511	    throw new Error(
512	      `Base "${config.base}" or icon library "${config.iconLibrary}" not found`
513	    )
514	  }
515	
516	  const registryTheme = buildRegistryTheme(config)
517	
518	  // Build dependencies.
519	  const dependencies = [
520	    `shadcn@${SHADCN_VERSION}`,
521	    "class-variance-authority",
522	    "tw-animate-css",
523	    ...(baseItem.dependencies ?? []),
524	    ...iconLibraryItem.packages,
525	  ]
526	
527	  const registryDependencies = ["utils"]
528	  const themeVars = {
529	    ...(registryTheme.cssVars?.theme ?? {}),
530	    ...(normalizedFontHeading === "inherit"
531	      ? { "--font-heading": getInheritedHeadingFontValue(config.font) }
532	      : {}),
533	  }
534	
535	  if (config.font) {
536	    registryDependencies.push(`font-${config.font}`)
537	  }
538	
539	  if (normalizedFontHeading !== "inherit") {
540	    registryDependencies.push(`font-heading-${normalizedFontHeading}`)
541	  }
542	
543	  return {
544	    name: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
545	    extends: "none",
546	    type: "registry:base" as const,
547	    config: {
548	      style: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
549	      iconLibrary: iconLibraryItem.name,
550	      rtl: config.rtl,
551	      menuColor: config.menuColor,
552	      menuAccent: config.menuAccent,
553	      tailwind: {
554	        baseColor: config.baseColor,
555	      },
556	    },
557	    dependencies,
558	    registryDependencies,
559	    cssVars: {
560	      ...registryTheme.cssVars,
561	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
562	    },
563	    css: {
564	      '@import "tw-animate-css"': {},
565	      '@import "shadcn/tailwind.css"': {},
566	      "@layer base": {
567	        "*": { "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {} },
568	        body: { "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {} },
569	      },
570	    },
571	    ...(config.rtl && {
572	      docs: `To learn how to set up the RTL provider and fonts for your app, see https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/rtl/${config.template === "next-monorepo" ? "next" : (config.template ?? "next")}`,
573	    }),
574	  }
575	}

1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thanks for your interest in contributing to ui.shadcn.com. We're happy to have you here.
4	
5	Please take a moment to review this document before submitting your first pull request. We also strongly recommend that you check for open issues and pull requests to see if someone else is working on something similar.
6	
7	If you need any help, feel free to reach out to [@shadcn](https://twitter.com/shadcn).
8	
9	## About this repository
10	
11	This repository is a monorepo.
12	
13	- We use [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) and [`workspaces`](https://pnpm.io/workspaces) for development.
14	- We use [Turborepo](https://turbo.build/repo) as our build system.
15	- We use [changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for managing releases.
16	
17	## Structure
18	
19	This repository is structured as follows:
20	
21	```
22	apps
23	└── v4
24	    ├── app
25	    ├── components
26	    ├── content
27	    └── registry
28	        └── new-york-v4
29	            ├── example
30	            └── ui
31	packages
32	└── shadcn
33	```
34	
35	| Path                 | Description                              |
36	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
37	| `apps/v4/app`        | The Next.js application for the website. |
38	| `apps/v4/components` | The React components for the website.    |
39	| `apps/v4/content`    | The content for the website.             |
40	| `apps/v4/registry`   | The registry for the components.         |
41	| `packages/shadcn`    | The `shadcn` package.                    |
42	
43	## Development
44	
45	### Fork this repo
46	
47	You can fork this repo by clicking the fork button in the top right corner of this page.
48	
49	### Clone on your local machine
50	
51	```bash
52	git clone https://github.com/your-username/ui.git
53	```
54	
55	### Navigate to project directory
56	
57	```bash
58	cd ui
59	```
60	
61	### Create a new Branch
62	
63	```bash
64	git checkout -b my-new-branch
65	```
66	
67	### Install dependencies
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	### Run a workspace
74	
75	You can use the `pnpm --filter=[WORKSPACE]` command to start the development process for a workspace.
76	
77	#### Examples
78	
79	1. To run the `ui.shadcn.com` website:
80	
81	```bash
82	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
83	```
84	
85	2. To run the `shadcn` package:
86	
87	```bash
88	pnpm --filter=shadcn dev
89	```
90	
91	## Running the CLI Locally
92	
93	To run the CLI locally, you can follow the workflow:
94	
95	1. Start by running the dev server:
96	
97	   ```bash
98	   pnpm dev
99	   ```
100	
101	2. In another terminal tab, test the CLI by running:
102	
103	   ```bash
104	   pnpm shadcn
105	   ```
106	
107	   To test the CLI in a specific app, use a command like:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   pnpm shadcn <init | add | ...> -c ~/Desktop/my-app
111	   ```
112	
113	This workflow ensures that you are running the most recent version of the registry and testing the CLI properly in your local environment.
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	The documentation for this project is located in the `v4` workspace. You can run the documentation locally by running the following command:
118	
119	```bash
120	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
121	```
122	
123	Documentation is written using [MDX](https://mdxjs.com). You can find the documentation files in the `apps/v4/content/docs` directory.
124	
125	## Components
126	
127	We use a registry system for developing components. You can find the source code for the components under `apps/v4/registry`. The components are organized by styles.
128	
129	```bash
130	apps
131	└── v4
132	    └── registry
133	        └── new-york-v4
134	            ├── example
135	            └── ui
136	```
137	
138	When adding or modifying components, please ensure that:
139	
140	1. You make the changes for every style.
141	2. You update the documentation.
142	3. You run `pnpm registry:build` to update the registry.
143	
144	## Commit Convention
145	
146	Before you create a Pull Request, please check whether your commits comply with
147	the commit conventions used in this repository.
148	
149	When you create a commit we kindly ask you to follow the convention
150	`category(scope or module): message` in your commit message while using one of
151	the following categories:
152	
153	- `feat / feature`: all changes that introduce completely new code or new
154	  features
155	- `fix`: changes that fix a bug (ideally you will additionally reference an
156	  issue if present)
157	- `refactor`: any code related change that is not a fix nor a feature
158	- `docs`: changing existing or creating new documentation (i.e. README, docs for
159	  usage of a lib or cli usage)
160	- `build`: all changes regarding the build of the software, changes to
161	  dependencies or the addition of new dependencies
162	- `test`: all changes regarding tests (adding new tests or changing existing
163	  ones)
164	- `ci`: all changes regarding the configuration of continuous integration (i.e.
165	  github actions, ci system)
166	- `chore`: all changes to the repository that do not fit into any of the above
167	  categories
168	
169	  e.g. `feat(components): add new prop to the avatar component`
170	
171	If you are interested in the detailed specification you can visit
172	https://www.conventionalcommits.org/ or check out the
173	[Angular Commit Message Guidelines](https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/22b96b9/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines).
174	
175	## Requests for new components
176	
177	If you have a request for a new component, please open a discussion on GitHub. We'll be happy to help you out.
178	
179	## CLI
180	
181	The `shadcn` package is a CLI for adding components to your project. You can find the documentation for the CLI [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli).
182	
183	Any changes to the CLI should be made in the `packages/shadcn` directory. If you can, it would be great if you could add tests for your changes.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	Tests are written using [Vitest](https://vitest.dev). You can run all the tests from the root of the repository.
188	
189	```bash
190	pnpm test
191	```
192	
193	Please ensure that the tests are passing when submitting a pull request. If you're adding new features, please include tests.
194

6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([

8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)

28	export const rawConfigSchema = z

30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),

23	
24	/*
25	 * build-registry.mts is the single v4 registry pipeline.
26	 *
27	 * Source of truth:
28	 * - Authored raw component/registry source lives in registry/bases/base and
29	 *   registry/bases/radix.
30	 * - Authored demo source lives in examples/base and examples/radix.
31	 * - Style tokens live in registry/styles/style-*.css.
32	 *
33	 * Persistent outputs:
34	 * - registry/bases/__index__.tsx
35	 * - registry/__index__.tsx
36	 * - examples/__index__.tsx
37	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui/*
38	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
39	 * - public/r/*
40	 *
41	 * Temporary outputs:
42	 * - registry/<base-style>/*
43	 * - registry-<style>.json
44	 *
45	 * Execution order:
46	 * 1. Build registry/bases/__index__.tsx from the authored base registries.
47	 * 2. Build temporary styled registries under registry/<base-style>.
48	 * 3. Build registry/__index__.tsx for runtime lookup across legacy styles and
49	 *    generated base-style combinations.

1	# Examples
2	
3	This directory contains demo components for the component documentation.
4	
5	## Directory Structure
6	
7	```
8	examples
9	├── base
10	│   ├── button-demo.tsx
11	│   └── ...
12	├── radix
13	│   └── ...
14	└── __index__.tsx    # Auto-generated by registry:build
15	```
16	
17	## Adding a New Example
18	
19	1. Create a new `.tsx` file in `examples/base` or `examples/radix`:
20	
21	```tsx
22	// examples/base/button-loading.tsx
23	import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button"
24	
25	export function ButtonLoading() {
26	  return <Button disabled>Loading...</Button>
27	}
28	```
29	
30	2. Run the registry build to regenerate the index:
31	
32	```bash
33	pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build
34	```
35	
36	3. Use the example in documentation by referencing its name (filename without `.tsx`):
37	
38	```tsx
39	<ComponentPreview name="button-loading" />
40	```
41	
42	## Notes
43	
44	- Example files should be placed directly in `examples/base` or `examples/radix`, not in subdirectories.
45	- Base examples should import from `@/styles/base-nova/*`. Radix examples should import from `@/styles/radix-nova/*`.
46	- Both named exports and default exports are supported.
47	- After adding or removing examples, run `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build` to update the index.
48

1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thanks for your interest in contributing to ui.shadcn.com. We're happy to have you here.
4	
5	Please take a moment to review this document before submitting your first pull request. We also strongly recommend that you check for open issues and pull requests to see if someone else is working on something similar.
6	
7	If you need any help, feel free to reach out to [@shadcn](https://twitter.com/shadcn).
8	
9	## About this repository
10	
11	This repository is a monorepo.
12	
13	- We use [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) and [`workspaces`](https://pnpm.io/workspaces) for development.
14	- We use [Turborepo](https://turbo.build/repo) as our build system.
15	- We use [changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for managing releases.
16	
17	## Structure
18	
19	This repository is structured as follows:
20	
21	```
22	apps
23	└── v4
24	    ├── app
25	    ├── components
26	    ├── content
27	    └── registry
28	        └── new-york-v4
29	            ├── example
30	            └── ui
31	packages
32	└── shadcn
33	```
34	
35	| Path                 | Description                              |
36	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
37	| `apps/v4/app`        | The Next.js application for the website. |
38	| `apps/v4/components` | The React components for the website.    |
39	| `apps/v4/content`    | The content for the website.             |
40	| `apps/v4/registry`   | The registry for the components.         |
41	| `packages/shadcn`    | The `shadcn` package.                    |
42	
43	## Development
44	
45	### Fork this repo
46	
47	You can fork this repo by clicking the fork button in the top right corner of this page.
48	
49	### Clone on your local machine
50	
51	```bash
52	git clone https://github.com/your-username/ui.git
53	```
54	
55	### Navigate to project directory
56	
57	```bash
58	cd ui
59	```
60	
61	### Create a new Branch
62	
63	```bash
64	git checkout -b my-new-branch
65	```
66	
67	### Install dependencies
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	### Run a workspace
74	
75	You can use the `pnpm --filter=[WORKSPACE]` command to start the development process for a workspace.
76	
77	#### Examples
78	
79	1. To run the `ui.shadcn.com` website:
80	
81	```bash
82	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
83	```
84	
85	2. To run the `shadcn` package:
86	
87	```bash
88	pnpm --filter=shadcn dev
89	```
90	
91	## Running the CLI Locally
92	
93	To run the CLI locally, you can follow the workflow:
94	
95	1. Start by running the dev server:
96	
97	   ```bash
98	   pnpm dev
99	   ```
100	
101	2. In another terminal tab, test the CLI by running:
102	
103	   ```bash
104	   pnpm shadcn
105	   ```
106	
107	   To test the CLI in a specific app, use a command like:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   pnpm shadcn <init | add | ...> -c ~/Desktop/my-app
111	   ```
112	
113	This workflow ensures that you are running the most recent version of the registry and testing the CLI properly in your local environment.
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	The documentation for this project is located in the `v4` workspace. You can run the documentation locally by running the following command:
118	
119	```bash
120	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
121	```
122	
123	Documentation is written using [MDX](https://mdxjs.com). You can find the documentation files in the `apps/v4/content/docs` directory.
124	
125	## Components
126	
127	We use a registry system for developing components. You can find the source code for the components under `apps/v4/registry`. The components are organized by styles.
128	
129	```bash
130	apps
131	└── v4
132	    └── registry
133	        └── new-york-v4
134	            ├── example
135	            └── ui
136	```
137	
138	When adding or modifying components, please ensure that:
139	
140	1. You make the changes for every style.
141	2. You update the documentation.
142	3. You run `pnpm registry:build` to update the registry.
143	
144	## Commit Convention
145	
146	Before you create a Pull Request, please check whether your commits comply with
147	the commit conventions used in this repository.
148	
149	When you create a commit we kindly ask you to follow the convention
150	`category(scope or module): message` in your commit message while using one of
151	the following categories:
152	
153	- `feat / feature`: all changes that introduce completely new code or new
154	  features
155	- `fix`: changes that fix a bug (ideally you will additionally reference an
156	  issue if present)
157	- `refactor`: any code related change that is not a fix nor a feature
158	- `docs`: changing existing or creating new documentation (i.e. README, docs for
159	  usage of a lib or cli usage)
160	- `build`: all changes regarding the build of the software, changes to
161	  dependencies or the addition of new dependencies
162	- `test`: all changes regarding tests (adding new tests or changing existing
163	  ones)
164	- `ci`: all changes regarding the configuration of continuous integration (i.e.
165	  github actions, ci system)
166	- `chore`: all changes to the repository that do not fit into any of the above
167	  categories
168	
169	  e.g. `feat(components): add new prop to the avatar component`
170	
171	If you are interested in the detailed specification you can visit
172	https://www.conventionalcommits.org/ or check out the
173	[Angular Commit Message Guidelines](https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/22b96b9/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines).
174	
175	## Requests for new components
176	
177	If you have a request for a new component, please open a discussion on GitHub. We'll be happy to help you out.
178	
179	## CLI
180	
181	The `shadcn` package is a CLI for adding components to your project. You can find the documentation for the CLI [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli).
182	
183	Any changes to the CLI should be made in the `packages/shadcn` directory. If you can, it would be great if you could add tests for your changes.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	Tests are written using [Vitest](https://vitest.dev). You can run all the tests from the root of the repository.
188	
189	```bash
190	pnpm test
191	```
192	
193	Please ensure that the tests are passing when submitting a pull request. If you're adding new features, please include tests.
194	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase structure. Let me create a detailed report with all the information you requested: ## Comprehensive Exploration Report: shadcn/ui Component Library Structure I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library codebase. Here are my findings: ### 1. **Demo Files Storage Locations** Demo files are stored in **two primary locations**: - **Authored demos**: `/apps/v4/examples/base/` and `/apps/v4/examples/radix/` - **Generated/registry versions**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` The authored demos in `examples/` are the source of truth, which are then processed by the build system to generate versioned copies in the registry folders. ### 2. **Naming Convention for Demo Files** Demo files follow a consistent pattern: - **Format**: `{component-name}-{variant-name}.tsx` - **Examples**: - `accordion-demo.tsx` (basic demo) - `accordion-borders.tsx` (variant) - `accordion-multiple.tsx` (variant) - `tabs-demo.tsx` - `checkbox-demo.tsx` - `button-group-dropdown.tsx` - `breadcrumb-responsive.tsx` Files are placed **directly in the examples folder** (not in subdirectories). ### 3. **Demo File Export Patterns** Demo files use **named or default exports** (both supported). Here are actual examples: **Example 1 - Default Export (TabsDemo)** ```tsx export function TabsDemo() { return ( {/* ... */} ) } ``` **Example 2 - Default Export with Named Function (CheckboxDemo)** ```tsx export default function CheckboxDemo() { return ( {/* ... */} ) } ``` **Example 3 - Client Component with State (BreadcrumbResponsive)** ```tsx "use client" import * as React from "react" // ... imports ... export default function BreadcrumbResponsive() { const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(false) const isDesktop = useMediaQuery("(min-width: 768px)") return ( {/* ... */} ) } ``` All demos: - Import components from `@/styles/base-nova/ui/*` or `@/styles/radix-nova/ui/*` - Use **default exports** (function-based) - Support both stateless and stateful components - Support "use client" directive when needed ### 4. **Registry Entry Structure for Examples vs. Components** **UI Component Registry Entry** (from `/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`): ```typescript { name: "accordion", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], files: [ { path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], } ``` **Example/Demo Registry Entry** (from `/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`): ```typescript { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], } ``` **Key Differences**: - UI entries: `type: "registry:ui"`, use `dependencies` for npm packages - Example entries: `type: "registry:example"`, use `registryDependencies` to reference other components - Examples depend on UI components via `registryDependencies` - Examples can also have multiple files (like toast which includes hooks) ### 5. **Index and Registry Files for Aggregation** **Key files that need updating when adding new components/demos**: 1. **Examples Index**: `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` - Auto-generated from source examples - Aggregates all demo files 2. **Registry Examples Index**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Manually maintained registry entries for examples - 235 entries (one per demo) 3. **UI Registry Index**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - 705 lines - Defines all UI component registry entries 4. **Main registry.json**: `/apps/v4/registry.json` - Generated JSON output of all components and examples **Build Script**: - `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` orchestrates the entire build process - Run with: `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build` ### 6. **Patterns for Compound Components** Compound components are demonstrated with all sub-components shown together: **Dialog Example** (DialogDemo): ```tsx export default function DialogDemo() { return ( Edit profile Make changes...
{/* ... */}
 ) } ``` **DropdownMenu Example** (shows all sub-components): ```tsx export default function DropdownMenuDemo() { return ( My Account Profile ⇧⌘P {/* More items */} Invite users {/* ... */} ) } ``` **UI Component (Accordion) with exported sub-components**: ```tsx function Accordion({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return } function AccordionItem({ className, ...props }: ...) { return } function AccordionTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: ...) { return {children}... } function AccordionContent({ className, children, ...props }: ...) { return
{children}
 } export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent } ``` Demos then import and use all these sub-components together to show the complete composition. ### 7. **Stepper Component Status** **Result**: No `stepper` component exists anywhere in the codebase yet. Searched for: - Files with "stepper" in filename - none found - References to "stepper" in content - none found This is a completely new component to add to the library. --- ### Key File Paths for Reference: - **Example source storage**: `/apps/v4/examples/base/` and `/apps/v4/examples/radix/` - **Example README**: `/apps/v4/examples/README.md` - **Registry config**: `/apps/v4/registry/config.ts` - **Example registry entries**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - **UI registry entries**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - **Build script**: `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` - **Contributing guide**: `/CONTRIBUTING.md`

Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide a comprehensive exploration report: ## Comprehensive Structure of shadcn/ui Component Library Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the complete breakdown: ### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure** **Root Level** (`/`): - **`apps/v4`** - Main Next.js documentation/website application - **`packages/shadcn`** - The CLI package (npmjs distribution) - **`scripts/`** - Global build scripts - **`templates/`** - Project templates for `shadcn init` (multiple frameworks) - **Configuration files**: `package.json`, `turbo.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `tsconfig.json` - **Monorepo tools**: pnpm (v9.0.6), Turborepo **Key Files**: - `package.json` - Root workspace definition with build commands like `registry:build`, `v4:dev` - `CONTRIBUTING.md` - Development guidelines --- ### 2. **Registry System - Deep Dive** The registry is a **TypeScript-based metadata system** that defines all components and their dependencies. Here's the complete structure: #### **Registry Schema** (in `packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`) All registry items follow a **Zod-validated schema** with discriminated unions. The main types are: ```typescript export type RegistryItem = z.infer ``` **Registry Item Types** (enum): - `registry:ui` - Individual UI components (button, alert, etc.) - `registry:block` - Multi-file complex components (dashboards, sidebars) - `registry:example` - Demo/example files - `registry:hook` - React hooks - `registry:lib` - Utility libraries - `registry:page` - Full pages (often used as targets in blocks) - `registry:component` - Sub-components within blocks - `registry:file` - Static files (JSON data, etc.) - `registry:theme` - Theme configuration - `registry:style` - Global styles - `registry:base` - Base configuration with theme/style - `registry:font` - Font family definitions - `registry:internal` - Internal/private components (not exported) #### **Common Registry Item Fields** Every registry item has these core fields: ```typescript { name: string; // Unique identifier (kebab-case) type: RegistryItemType; // One of the types above title?: string; // Display name description?: string; // Human-readable description author?: string; // Component author // Dependencies dependencies?: string[]; // npm packages (e.g., "radix-ui", "date-fns@latest") devDependencies?: string[]; // Dev-only packages registryDependencies?: string[]; // Other registry items required // Files array - CRITICAL files: Array<{ path: string; // Source path in registry type: RegistryItemType; // File-specific type target?: string; // Where to place in user's project content?: string; // Optional inline content }>; // Styling tailwind?: { config?: { content?: string[]; theme?: Record; plugins?: string[]; }; }; cssVars?: { theme?: Record; // Neutral theme variables light?: Record; // Light mode overrides dark?: Record; // Dark mode overrides }; css?: Record; // Raw CSS/PostCSS objects // Other metadata categories?: string[]; // e.g., ["dashboard", "forms"] docs?: string; // Link to documentation meta?: Record; // Custom metadata registryDependencies?: string[]; } ``` #### **Special Cases** **For `registry:base` items:** ```typescript { type: "registry:base"; extends?: string; // Can extend another base config: Partial; // Includes style, iconLibrary, etc. } ``` **For `registry:font` items:** ```typescript { type: "registry:font"; font: { family: string; provider: "google"; import: string; variable: string; // CSS variable name weight?: string[]; subsets?: string[]; }; } ``` --- ### 3. **Component Storage Structure** **Location**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/` (can have multiple style variants) ``` new-york-v4/ ├── ui/ # UI components │ ├── button.tsx │ ├── alert.tsx │ ├── accordion.tsx │ ├── _registry.ts # Exports: Registry["items"] │ └── ... ├── blocks/ # Complex multi-file components │ ├── dashboard-01/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Main page component (registry:page) │ │ ├── data.json # Static data (registry:file) │ │ └── components/ │ │ ├── app-sidebar.tsx │ │ ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx │ │ └── ... │ ├── sidebar-01/ │ └── _registry.ts ├── examples/ # Example/demo components │ ├── accordion-demo.tsx │ ├── alert-demo.tsx │ ├── _registry.ts │ └── ... ├── hooks/ # React hooks │ ├── use-mobile.ts │ └── _registry.ts ├── lib/ # Utility libraries │ ├── utils.ts │ └── _registry.ts ├── internal/ # Internal components (not public) │ ├── sidebar-menu.tsx │ └── _registry.ts ├── charts/ # Chart-specific components │ └── _registry.ts └── registry.ts # Main registry aggregator ``` --- ### 4. **Registry Files - How They Work** Each category has a `_registry.ts` file that exports an array of registry items: **Example: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const ui: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "button", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], files: [ { path: "ui/button.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, // ... more items ] ``` **Example with dependencies: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/_registry.ts`** ```typescript { name: "dashboard-01", type: "registry:block", description: "A dashboard with sidebar, charts and data table.", dependencies: [ "@dnd-kit/core", "zod", // ... external npm packages ], registryDependencies: [ "sidebar", // Depends on another registry item "breadcrumb", "chart", // ... other registry items ], files: [ { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx", // Where it installs in user project }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/data.json", type: "registry:file", target: "app/dashboard/data.json", }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, // ... more component files ], categories: ["dashboard"], meta: { iframeHeight: "1000px", // Custom metadata for rendering }, } ``` **Example with tailwind config and CSS vars:** ```typescript { name: "sidebar", type: "registry:ui", // ... tailwind: { config: { theme: { extend: { colors: { sidebar: { DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--sidebar-background))", foreground: "hsl(var(--sidebar-foreground))", // ... more variables }, }, }, }, }, }, cssVars: { light: { "sidebar-background": "0 0% 98%", "sidebar-foreground": "0 0% 0%", // ... more variables }, dark: { "sidebar-background": "0 0% 8%", "sidebar-foreground": "0 0% 100%", }, }, } ``` --- ### 5. **Main Registry Aggregator** **File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` ```typescript import { registryItemSchema, type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" import { ui } from "./ui/_registry" import { blocks } from "./blocks/_registry" import { examples } from "./examples/_registry" import { hooks } from "./hooks/_registry" import { lib } from "./lib/_registry" import { charts } from "./charts/_registry" import { internal } from "./internal/_registry" import { themes } from "../_legacy-themes" export const registry = { name: "shadcn/ui", homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com", items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([ // Style definition { name: "index", type: "registry:style", ... }, // All items from sub-registries ...ui, ...blocks, ...charts, ...lib, ...hooks, ...themes, ...examples, ...internal, ].filter((item) => !DEPRECATED_ITEMS.includes(item.name))) } satisfies Registry ``` --- ### 6. **Demo/Example Files Structure** Examples are stored alongside components: **File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/alert-demo.tsx` ```typescript import { AlertCircleIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Alert, AlertDescription, AlertTitle } from content.text:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide a comprehensive exploration report: ## Comprehensive Structure of shadcn/ui Component Library Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the complete breakdown: ### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure** **Root Level** (`/`): - **`apps/v4`** - Main Next.js documentation/website application - **`packages/shadcn`** - The CLI package (npmjs distribution) - **`scripts/`** - Global build scripts - **`templates/`** - Project templates for `shadcn init` (multiple frameworks) - **Configuration files**: `package.json`, `turbo.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `tsconfig.json` - **Monorepo tools**: pnpm (v9.0.6), Turborepo **Key Files**: - `package.json` - Root workspace definition with build commands like `registry:build`, `v4:dev` - `CONTRIBUTING.md` - Development guidelines --- ### 2. **Registry System - Deep Dive** The registry is a **TypeScript-based metadata system** that defines all components and their dependencies. Here's the complete structure: #### **Registry Schema** (in `packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`) All registry items follow a **Zod-validated schema** with discriminated unions. The main types are: ```typescript export type RegistryItem = z.infer ``` **Registry Item Types** (enum): - `registry:ui` - Individual UI components (button, alert, etc.) - `registry:block` - Multi-file complex components (dashboards, sidebars) - `registry:example` - Demo/example files - `registry:hook` - React hooks - `registry:lib` - Utility libraries - `registry:page` - Full pages (often used as targets in blocks) - `registry:component` - Sub-components within blocks - `registry:file` - Static files (JSON data, etc.) - `registry:theme` - Theme configuration - `registry:style` - Global styles - `registry:base` - Base configuration with theme/style - `registry:font` - Font family definitions - `registry:internal` - Internal/private components (not exported) #### **Common Registry Item Fields** Every registry item has these core fields: ```typescript { name: string; // Unique identifier (kebab-case) type: RegistryItemType; // One of the types above title?: string; // Display name description?: string; // Human-readable description author?: string; // Component author // Dependencies dependencies?: string[]; // npm packages (e.g., "radix-ui", "date-fns@latest") devDependencies?: string[]; // Dev-only packages registryDependencies?: string[]; // Other registry items required // Files array - CRITICAL files: Array<{ path: string; // Source path in registry type: RegistryItemType; // File-specific type target?: string; // Where to place in user's project content?: string; // Optional inline content }>; // Styling tailwind?: { config?: { content?: string[]; theme?: Record; plugins?: string[]; }; }; cssVars?: { theme?: Record; // Neutral theme variables light?: Record; // Light mode overrides dark?: Record; // Dark mode overrides }; css?: Record; // Raw CSS/PostCSS objects // Other metadata categories?: string[]; // e.g., ["dashboard", "forms"] docs?: string; // Link to documentation meta?: Record; // Custom metadata registryDependencies?: string[]; } ``` #### **Special Cases** **For `registry:base` items:** ```typescript { type: "registry:base"; extends?: string; // Can extend another base config: Partial; // Includes style, iconLibrary, etc. } ``` **For `registry:font` items:** ```typescript { type: "registry:font"; font: { family: string; provider: "google"; import: string; variable: string; // CSS variable name weight?: string[]; subsets?: string[]; }; } ``` --- ### 3. **Component Storage Structure** **Location**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/` (can have multiple style variants) ``` new-york-v4/ ├── ui/ # UI components │ ├── button.tsx │ ├── alert.tsx │ ├── accordion.tsx │ ├── _registry.ts # Exports: Registry["items"] │ └── ... ├── blocks/ # Complex multi-file components │ ├── dashboard-01/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Main page component (registry:page) │ │ ├── data.json # Static data (registry:file) │ │ └── components/ │ │ ├── app-sidebar.tsx │ │ ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx │ │ └── ... │ ├── sidebar-01/ │ └── _registry.ts ├── examples/ # Example/demo components │ ├── accordion-demo.tsx │ ├── alert-demo.tsx │ ├── _registry.ts │ └── ... ├── hooks/ # React hooks │ ├── use-mobile.ts │ └── _registry.ts ├── lib/ # Utility libraries │ ├── utils.ts │ └── _registry.ts ├── internal/ # Internal components (not public) │ ├── sidebar-menu.tsx │ └── _registry.ts ├── charts/ # Chart-specific components │ └── _registry.ts └── registry.ts # Main registry aggregator ``` --- ### 4. **Registry Files - How They Work** Each category has a `_registry.ts` file that exports an array of registry items: **Example: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** ```typescript import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" export const ui: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "button", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], files: [ { path: "ui/button.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, // ... more items ] ``` **Example with dependencies: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/_registry.ts`** ```typescript { name: "dashboard-01", type: "registry:block", description: "A dashboard with sidebar, charts and data table.", dependencies: [ "@dnd-kit/core", "zod", // ... external npm packages ], registryDependencies: [ "sidebar", // Depends on another registry item "breadcrumb", "chart", // ... other registry items ], files: [ { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx", type: "registry:page", target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx", // Where it installs in user project }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/data.json", type: "registry:file", target: "app/dashboard/data.json", }, { path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx", type: "registry:component", }, // ... more component files ], categories: ["dashboard"], meta: { iframeHeight: "1000px", // Custom metadata for rendering }, } ``` **Example with tailwind config and CSS vars:** ```typescript { name: "sidebar", type: "registry:ui", // ... tailwind: { config: { theme: { extend: { colors: { sidebar: { DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--sidebar-background))", foreground: "hsl(var(--sidebar-foreground))", // ... more variables }, }, }, }, }, }, cssVars: { light: { "sidebar-background": "0 0% 98%", "sidebar-foreground": "0 0% 0%", // ... more variables }, dark: { "sidebar-background": "0 0% 8%", "sidebar-foreground": "0 0% 100%", }, }, } ``` --- ### 5. **Main Registry Aggregator** **File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` ```typescript import { registryItemSchema, type Registry } from "shadcn/schema" import { ui } from "./ui/_registry" import { blocks } from "./blocks/_registry" import { examples } from "./examples/_registry" import { hooks } from "./hooks/_registry" import { lib } from "./lib/_registry" import { charts } from "./charts/_registry" import { internal } from "./internal/_registry" import { themes } from "../_legacy-themes" export const registry = { name: "shadcn/ui", homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com", items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([ // Style definition { name: "index", type: "registry:style", ... }, // All items from sub-registries ...ui, ...blocks, ...charts, ...lib, ...hooks, ...themes, ...examples, ...internal, ].filter((item) => !DEPRECATED_ITEMS.includes(item.name))) } satisfies Registry ``` --- ### 6. **Demo/Example Files Structure** Examples are stored alongside components: **File**: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/alert-demo.tsx` ```typescript import { AlertCircleIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Alert, AlertDescription, AlertTitle } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/alert" export default function AlertDemo() { return (
Error Your session has expired.
) } ``` **Registry Entry** (from `examples/_registry.ts`): ```typescript { name: "alert-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["alert"], // Depends on alert component files: [ { path: "examples/alert-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], } ``` --- ### 7. **Key Configuration Files** #### **`/apps/v4/components.json`** (shadcn config) ```json { "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json", "style": "new-york", "rsc": true, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "", "css": "app/globals.css", "baseColor": "neutral", "cssVariables": true, "prefix": "" }, "aliases": { "components": "@/components", "utils": "@/lib/utils", "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide" } ``` #### **`/packages/shadcn/package.json`** - Entry point: `./dist/index.js` (CLI) - Exports: - `"."` - Main CLI - `"./registry"` - Registry API - `"./schema"` - Zod schemas - `"./mcp"` - Model Context Protocol - `"./utils"` - Utility functions - `"./icons"` - Icon libraries metadata - `"./preset"` - Preset definitions - `"./tailwind.css"` - Tailwind CSS file #### **`/apps/v4/package.json`** - Scripts: - `registry:build` - Generates registry files - `registry:capture` - Captures latest registry - `dev` - Development server - `build` - Production build --- ### 8. **Build System** **Build Pipeline** (`/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts`): 1. **Build registry bases** → generates `registry/bases/__index__.tsx` 2. **Build styled registries** → temporary `registry//` 3. **Build main registry index** → `registry/__index__.tsx` (lazy-loaded components) 4. **Build examples index** → `examples/__index__.tsx` 5. **Export public registries** → `public/r/*` (through shadcn CLI) 6. **Copy compiled UI components** → `styles/

Setting up the context structure...

Now I'm realizing the "completed" state depends on knowing the step order, but since children define the order in this pattern, I need to figure out how to track that. The `Stepper` component has the active step value, and each `StepperItem` declares its step identifier, but without a predefined steps array, determining which steps are completed versus upcoming becomes tricky. I could require users to pass a steps array to the Stepper, or find another way to infer the order from the rendered children. I think the cleanest approach is to use numeric step values—the `Stepper` component accepts a `value` prop for the current step, each `StepperItem` has its own `step` prop, and I can determine completion status by comparing them (completed if step < value, active if step === value). This keeps the API simple and intuitive for wizard-style flows. I'm settling on using numeric step values—each `StepperItem` gets a `step` number, the `Stepper` tracks the current `value` as a number, and completion logic is straightforward: a step is completed if its number is less than the current value, and active if it matches. This keeps the API minimal and intuitive without needing a separate steps array. I'm settling on using a `steps` array to define the step order, with string-based values for each step ID—this mirrors how Tabs and Accordion work in the library. The Stepper component will accept `value` for the active step, `onValueChange` for updates, and `steps` as an ordered list to determine which steps are completed, plus `orientation` for layout direction. I'm checking how other libraries handle steppers to make sure my approach is solid—they all use integer-based step tracking, which confirms that's the right direction. Now I'm thinking through the actual component layout, particularly how the visual separators between steps should be structured in the DOM. I'm defining the individual components that make up the stepper: the trigger button with its title and description text, the separator lines between steps, and the content panels that display when each step is active. The user will compose these pieces together, and I'm showing an example of how they'd structure a horizontal stepper with multiple steps, each containing a trigger with labeled sections and separators.

Now I'm realizing the `StepperPanel` needs access to the stepper's context to know which step is active, so it should live inside `StepperItem` rather than floating outside—that way it can properly respond to value changes and render its content conditionally. Building the stepper component structure...

I'm realizing the nested panel approach gets messy with horizontal layouts, so for the demo I'll keep the content panels separate and render them below the stepper navigation instead of nesting them inside each item.

For the actual component API, I want to keep it clean without extra wrapper layers—so I'll have Stepper as the context provider, then StepperItem for each step with its trigger (containing title and optional description), a separator between items, and StepperPanel as a convenience component that conditionally renders content based on the active step. This way the panels can live anywhere inside the Stepper context without needing a dedicated StepperList wrapper. I'm deciding on the state management approach—I'll support both controlled and uncontrolled modes by accepting optional `value`, `defaultValue`, and `onValueChange` props. I'm also defining the TypeScript types for the Stepper context (tracking the current step, orientation) and StepperItem context (tracking which step is active and completed).

For the layout, horizontal orientation stacks triggers with separators stretching between them, while vertical orientation alternates between triggers, panels, and separators in a column. Now I'm ready to start writing the actual component code...

Writing component structure... Writing the Stepper component... I'm defining the StepperItem component that accepts a step number and manages its active and completed states based on the current value from context, then wrapping it in a provider to pass those states down to child elements. Now I'm thinking through the horizontal layout behavior...

For horizontal layouts, I want the trigger and separator to sit inline with flex-row, and the separator should expand to fill space between items. In vertical mode, the trigger goes at the top, the panel content in the middle, and the separator stretches below. The StepperItem needs to contain the trigger, separator, and optional panel, with the layout direction changing based on orientation.

Now I'm considering the StepperTrigger component...

Writing trigger component... Writing component styles... Writing component definitions...

Now I'm reconsidering whether `StepperPanel` should completely remove itself from the DOM when inactive, or if it should stay in the DOM but be hidden for accessibility purposes—keeping the element present might be better for screen readers and keyboard navigation.

Looking at how Accordion and Tabs handle this with Radix, they manage visibility through ARIA attributes and CSS rather than conditional rendering. Since I'm building this without Radix, the simplest approach that aligns with how Accordion works is to just return null when the step isn't active.

I'm starting to sketch out the demo file structure with the component imports and setting up an array of step definitions.

Building the demo component... Writing component navigation...

I'm realizing the `StepperSeparator` needs access to context about whether the previous step is completed, which means it either needs to live inside `StepperItem` to access that context, or I need to pass completion state as a prop from the parent. The cleanest approach is probably placing the separator inside each `StepperItem` after the trigger, so it naturally knows the completion state of its parent step and can style itself accordingly. I'm settling on a cleaner approach: place `StepperSeparator` inside `StepperItem` right after the trigger, where it can access both the orientation from context and the completed state from the item. For the last step, users simply omit the separator. This keeps the component API straightforward and matches the "separators between steps" pattern the user described.

Writing the final API structure... Defining the StepperPanel component... I'm realizing there's a layout problem here—if the `Stepper` container uses flexbox row for horizontal orientation, the `StepperPanel` components would also flow horizontally, which breaks the intended vertical stacking of content. I need to restructure this so the panels are positioned separately from the navigation items.

The cleanest approach is to introduce a `StepperList` wrapper that handles the row/column layout for the steps themselves, keeping the outer `Stepper` as a flex column container that can hold both the navigation and content panels in the right order. This way the panels naturally sit below the step indicators without fighting the flex direction. I'm realizing the cleanest approach is to let users compose the layout themselves—they can wrap the step items in a flex row for horizontal layout and the panels below, giving them full control over the structure without forcing a specific DOM hierarchy. Building the stepper component structure... Writing the demo layout... I'll go with the horizontal demo since that's the most common pattern for onboarding flows. I'm building out the stepper structure with `StepperItem` components that include triggers and separators, and I'll demonstrate how `StepperPanel` works within that context.

Actually, I'm reconsidering the approach—maybe I should show a vertical layout instead since `StepperPanel` makes more sense when the content is displayed inline. Or I could do both, but that might be too much. Let me just create one clean horizontal demo and mention the vertical option in a comment.

For `StepperPanel`, I'm thinking about making it flexible by adding an optional `step` prop so it can work both inside and outside `StepperItem`. That way it reads from the appropriate context depending on where it's used, and I can document the error handling if someone tries to use it incorrectly.

Defining the component structure...

I'm mapping out the files I need to create and which registry entries to add. The stepper component goes in the UI folder, the demo example in the examples folder, and I'll need to add entries to both registry files to make them discoverable. The user was clear about not touching existing component files, so I'm just creating new ones and updating the registries. Now I'm documenting the key design decisions for the stepper component—using a custom React context instead of Radix UI, supporting both controlled and uncontrolled modes with integer step values, and leveraging Tailwind's group selectors and data attributes for styling without needing a variant library.

# Plan: Add "Stepper" Compound Component to shadcn/ui ## Context Multi-step wizards and onboarding flows are a common UI pattern not yet covered by the shadcn/ui library. The goal is to add a `Stepper` compound component that follows the exact same patterns used throughout the codebase — React context for shared state, Tailwind data-attribute selectors for orientation/state styling, the `cn()` utility, `data-slot` attributes, and controlled/uncontrolled support. No existing component is modified. Only **2 new files** are created and **2 registry files** receive new entries. --- ## Files to Create ### 1. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` ### 2. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` ## Files to Modify (registry entries only) ### 3. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` — insert between the `spinner` entry (line 554) and `switch` entry (line 565) ### 4. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` — insert between the `spinner-item` entry (line 2115) and `switch-demo` entry (line 2126) The main `registry.ts` aggregator already imports from both `_registry.ts` files via spread (`...ui`, `...examples`), so no further changes are needed there. --- ## Component Design: `stepper.tsx` ### Sub-components (all exported at the bottom) | Sub-component | HTML element | Role | |--------------------|-------------|------| | `Stepper` | `
` | Outer container; provides `StepperContext` | | `StepperItem` | `
` | Wraps one step; provides `StepperItemContext` | | `StepperTrigger` | `
) } ``` > Note: `StepperSeparator` is placed outside `StepperItem` in this demo (between items in > the `Stepper` flex-row). Because it's outside an item, it only reads from `StepperContext` > for orientation. We'll remove the `data-completed` attribute on the separator in this > configuration, OR we keep it inside `StepperItem` and adjust the demo layout accordingly. > (Final implementation will decide based on which layout renders cleanest.) --- ## Registry Entry: `ui/_registry.ts` Insert after the `spinner` entry block (after line 563), before `switch` (line 564): ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["lucide-react"], files: [ { path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, ``` > `lucide-react` is already a workspace dependency (used by accordion, etc.), so it > doesn't add a new install burden. --- ## Registry Entry: `examples/_registry.ts` Insert after `spinner-item` entry (after line 2124), before `switch-demo` (line 2125): ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, ``` --- ## Implementation Steps 1. **Create `stepper.tsx`** - File header: `"use client"`, imports (`React`, `CheckIcon`, `cn`) - Define `StepperContextValue` type and `StepperContext` with `React.createContext` - Define `useStepper()` hook (throws if outside provider) - Define `StepperItemContextValue` type and `StepperItemContext` - Define `useStepperItem()` hook (throws if outside provider) - Implement `Stepper` (handles controlled/uncontrolled, provides context, renders `
`) - Implement `StepperItem` (reads from `StepperContext`, provides `StepperItemContext`) - Implement `StepperTrigger` (reads both contexts, renders step indicator circle + `children`) - Implement `StepperTitle` (reads `StepperItemContext` for active/completed styling) - Implement `StepperDescription` (reads `StepperItemContext` for muted styling) - Implement `StepperSeparator` (reads both contexts for orientation + completed styling) - Implement `StepperPanel` (reads `StepperContext` + optional `step` prop or `StepperItemContext`, returns null when not active) - Named `export { ... }` at the bottom 2. **Create `stepper-demo.tsx`** - `"use client"` + imports from `button` and `stepper` - 3-step wizard with account / profile / review steps - Show both `StepperDescription` and `StepperTitle` in triggers - Include `StepperSeparator` between items - Use `StepperPanel` for step content - Include Previous / Next `Button` controls 3. **Edit `ui/_registry.ts`** - Insert the stepper registry entry (9 lines) between `spinner` and `switch` 4. **Edit `examples/_registry.ts`** - Insert the stepper-demo registry entry (11 lines) between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo` --- ## Verification 1. **TypeScript**: Run `pnpm --filter=v4 tsc --noEmit` — should produce no new errors 2. **Registry build**: Run `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build` — should complete without schema validation errors and emit `public/r/stepper.json` and `public/r/stepper-demo.json` 3. **Dev server**: Run `pnpm --filter=v4 dev`, navigate to the components page, confirm the stepper demo renders with correct horizontal layout, step state (incomplete / active / completed), and Previous / Next navigation works 4. **Manual render check**: - Step 1 active: circle `1` highlighted, steps 2/3 muted, separator after step 1 muted - Step 2 active: step 1 shows checkmark in filled circle, step 2 highlighted, step 3 muted, separator after step 1 is `bg-primary` - Step 3 active: steps 1 and 2 show checkmarks, step 3 highlighted, both separators colored

content.text:
Here's a summary of my analysis and plan:

---

## What I found

The repo is an `apps/v4` Next.js monorepo with a Zod-validated registry system. Components live in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`, demos in `…/examples/`. Each category has a `_registry.ts` that gets spread into the main `registry.ts` automatically — so I only need to touch **two existing files** (just to append new entries) and **create two new files**.

Key patterns confirmed by reading `accordion.tsx`, `tabs.tsx`, `separator.tsx`, and `form.tsx`:
- `"use client"` directive
- `data-slot="…"` on every element
- `data-orientation` + Tailwind `data-[orientation=…]:` selectors for layout switching
- `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for class merging
- `React.createContext` + custom `use*()` hooks that throw meaningful errors
- Named exports at the bottom of the file

---

## The plan (4 changes total)

| # | Action | File |
|---|--------|------|
| 1 | **Create** | `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` |
| 2 | **Create** | `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` |
| 3 | **Add entry** | `registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (between `spinner` and `switch`) |
| 4 | **Add entry** | `registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo`) |

**`stepper.tsx`** exports 7 sub-components: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperPanel`. State is integer-based (`value: number`), supports controlled + uncontrolled modes, shares state via two React contexts (`StepperContext` at the stepper level, `StepperItemContext` at the item level). No new npm dependencies — only `lucide-react` (already in workspace) and `@/lib/utils`.

**`stepper-demo.tsx`** is a `"use client"` 3-step wizard (Account → Profile → Review) with clickable step triggers, animated separator color transitions, `StepperPanel` content areas, and Previous/Next `Button` controls.

We'll remove the `data-completed` attribute on the separator in this > configuration, OR we keep it inside `StepperItem` and adjust the demo layout accordingly. > (Final implementation will decide based on which layout renders cleanest.) --- ## Registry Entry: `ui/_registry.ts` Insert after the `spinner` entry block (after line 563), before `switch` (line 564): ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["lucide-react"], files: [ { path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, ``` > `lucide-react` is already a workspace dependency (used by accordion, etc.), so it > doesn't add a new install burden. --- ## Registry Entry: `examples/_registry.ts` Insert after `spinner-item` entry (after line 2124), before `switch-demo` (line 2125): ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, ``` --- ## Implementation Steps 1. **Create `stepper.tsx`** - File header: `"use client"`, imports (`React`, `CheckIcon`, `cn`) - Define `StepperContextValue` type and `StepperContext` with `React.createContext` - Define `useStepper()` hook (throws if outside provider) - Define `StepperItemContextValue` type and `StepperItemContext` - Define `useStepperItem()` hook (throws if outside provider) - Implement `Stepper` (handles controlled/uncontrolled, provides context, renders `
`) - Implement `StepperItem` (reads from `StepperContext`, provides `StepperItemContext`) - Implement `StepperTrigger` (reads both contexts, renders step indicator circle + `children`) - Implement `StepperTitle` (reads `StepperItemContext` for active/completed styling) - Implement `StepperDescription` (reads `StepperItemContext` for muted styling) - Implement `StepperSeparator` (reads both contexts for orientation + completed styling) - Implement `StepperPanel` (reads `StepperContext` + optional `step` prop or `StepperItemContext`, returns null when not active) - Named `export { ... }` at the bottom 2. **Create `stepper-demo.tsx`** - `"use client"` + imports from `button` and `stepper` - 3-step wizard with account / profile / review steps - Show both `StepperDescription` and `StepperTitle` in triggers - Include `StepperSeparator` between items - Use `StepperPanel` for step content - Include Previous / Next `Button` controls 3. **Edit `ui/_registry.ts`** - Insert the stepper registry entry (9 lines) between `spinner` and `switch` 4. **Edit `examples/_registry.ts`** - Insert the stepper-demo registry entry (11 lines) between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo` --- ## Verification 1. **TypeScript**: Run `pnpm --filter=v4 tsc --noEmit` — should produce no new errors 2. **Registry build**: Run `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build` — should complete without schema validation errors and emit `public/r/stepper.json` and `public/r/stepper-demo.json` 3. **Dev server**: Run `pnpm --filter=v4 dev`, navigate to the components page, confirm the stepper demo renders with correct horizontal layout, step state (incomplete / active / completed), and Previous / Next navigation works 4. **Manual render check**: - Step 1 active: circle `1` highlighted, steps 2/3 muted, separator after step 1 muted - Step 2 active: step 1 shows checkmark in filled circle, step 2 highlighted, step 3 muted, separator after step 1 is `bg-primary` - Step 3 active: steps 1 and 2 show checkmarks, step 3 highlighted, both separators colored

file.content:
Here's a summary of my analysis and plan:

---

## What I found

The repo is an `apps/v4` Next.js monorepo with a Zod-validated registry system. Components live in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`, demos in `…/examples/`. Each category has a `_registry.ts` that gets spread into the main `registry.ts` automatically — so I only need to touch **two existing files** (just to append new entries) and **create two new files**.

Key patterns confirmed by reading `accordion.tsx`, `tabs.tsx`, `separator.tsx`, and `form.tsx`:
- `"use client"` directive
- `data-slot="…"` on every element
- `data-orientation` + Tailwind `data-[orientation=…]:` selectors for layout switching
- `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for class merging
- `React.createContext` + custom `use*()` hooks that throw meaningful errors
- Named exports at the bottom of the file

---

## The plan (4 changes total)

| # | Action | File |
|---|--------|------|
| 1 | **Create** | `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` |
| 2 | **Create** | `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` |
| 3 | **Add entry** | `registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (between `spinner` and `switch`) |
| 4 | **Add entry** | `registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo`) |

**`stepper.tsx`** exports 7 sub-components: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperPanel`. State is integer-based (`value: number`), supports controlled + uncontrolled modes, shares state via two React contexts (`StepperContext` at the stepper level, `StepperItemContext` at the item level). No new npm dependencies — only `lucide-react` (already in workspace) and `@/lib/utils`.

**`stepper-demo.tsx`** is a `"use client"` 3-step wizard (Account → Profile → Review) with clickable step triggers, animated separator color transitions, `StepperPanel` content areas, and Previous/Next `Button` controls.

9	  {

41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

Now I have all the context I need. Let me implement the plan step by step:

1. Create `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`
2. Create `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`

4. Add entry to `registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo`)

Let me now write the stepper component. Based on the patterns I see:


- I'll use the `"use client"` directive and add `data-slot` attributes throughout
- I'll leverage `data-orientation` with Tailwind selectors to handle vertical and horizontal layouts
- I'll set up a context with custom hooks to manage state across the stepper components
- The component structure will include a main `Stepper` container, individual `StepperItem` elements that track their position, clickable `StepperTrigger` buttons, `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` for text content, and `StepperSeparator` for visual dividers between steps

Writing the stepper component... Setting up the component structure... Writing the Stepper component... Writing component structure...

I'm thinking through how the StepperItem should be organized—in horizontal mode it displays steps in a row with connecting lines, while vertical mode stacks them with vertical connectors. The typical pattern has StepperItem as a container with child components like StepperTrigger for the clickable circle element. Building the component structure... I'm realizing the separator should sit between items rather than inside them. For a horizontal layout, I'd structure it with the stepper container using flexbox, then alternate between StepperItem components and StepperSeparator components to create that connected visual flow.

Writing the component code... Writing the Stepper component... Building the StepperItem component...

Now I'm creating the trigger button that advances to the current step when clicked, using the stepper context to handle the state change. After that comes the title component for displaying step labels.

Writing component definitions... Finishing the StepperPanel component that conditionally renders based on whether its step matches the active value, then exporting all the stepper components. I'm noticing the StepperSeparator relies on useStepperItem to determine completion state, which could be an issue depending on the component hierarchy. Building the horizontal layout...

The separator needs to live as a sibling within the Stepper container so it can track completion state from the parent, rather than nesting it inside StepperItem where the title and description placement becomes awkward. I'm settling on having StepperSeparator as a direct child of Stepper that reads context from the current step value to determine if the previous step is complete. I'm reconsidering the component structure—these should all be composable sub-components that users can arrange flexibly. The StepperTrigger will hold the indicator, title, and description together, while the StepperSeparator lives inside StepperItem so it can access the completion state and stretch horizontally between steps. Looking at a reference implementation, I can see the separator sits between items as a sibling element rather than nested inside them. The key challenge is determining whether the separator should be styled as completed or not—I could either have it check the previous item's state through context, or pass that information directly to the separator component.

I'm thinking the cleanest approach is to nest the separator inside the StepperItem itself, positioning it absolutely in horizontal mode so it extends beyond the item's bounds. For vertical layouts, the structure would be simpler with the trigger on the left and content flowing below.

Actually, let me simplify this. I'll design the component so the trigger displays the step indicator, and the title and description sit alongside or below it depending on whether we're in horizontal or vertical mode. The StepperItem will handle both orientations cleanly without overcomplicating the structure.

For the actual component API, I'm breaking it down into composable pieces: Stepper as the root container, StepperItem for each step, StepperTrigger for the clickable indicator, StepperTitle and StepperDescription for text content, StepperSeparator for the connecting line, and StepperPanel for the step content. The separator can check the StepperItem context to know if it should be highlighted as completed.

In horizontal mode, the Stepper uses flexbox in a row, each StepperItem is a column that takes up equal space, and the separator gets positioned to connect them visually.

Now I'm rethinking the layout structure—in horizontal mode, the trigger and separator should sit side-by-side within each item, with the separator expanding to fill the remaining space. For vertical mode, the separator sits below the trigger and connects downward to the next step. I'll implement both approaches and show them in the demo. Building the component structure... I'm deciding whether to stick with the seven components from the plan or add a `StepperIndicator` sub-component for the step number/check icon—I think keeping it to the planned seven is cleaner, and I can handle the indicator styling inline in the demo instead. Now I need to figure out the structure: should `StepperPanel` sit inside or outside `StepperItem`? For horizontal steppers, placing it outside makes more sense so all the content flows together naturally.

Now I'll write the `stepper.tsx` component following the established patterns: using the `"use client"` directive, adding `data-slot` attributes throughout, handling orientation with `data-orientation` and Tailwind selectors, leveraging `cn()` for class merging, and setting up React context with custom hooks that provide clear error messages. The component will consist of a `Stepper` container that manages state, a `StepperItem` for individual steps, and supporting subcomponents.

Writing component code... Setting up context providers...

Now I'm building the main Stepper component with controlled and uncontrolled value handling, then counting the total steps from the children to pass through context. I'm realizing `React.Children.count` won't work here since it counts all direct children including separators, not just the actual step items. I think the cleaner approach is to have users pass a `totalSteps` prop or derive it from the `step` values on each `StepperItem`. Let me reconsider the API design where each step item has a numeric `step` prop, and I can determine completion and active states based on comparing that to the current value.

Writing the component structure... horizontal layout would be:
- Steps in a row (flex-row)
- Separator is a horizontal line
- The panel content is below all steps

Actually looking at stepper UI patterns, there are different ways to structure it:
1. Header row with all step indicators, then content below
2. Each step item has its own content panel (accordion-like)

I'll go with option 2 (each step has its own panel), which is more flexible. The layout would be:
- Horizontal: steps laid out left to right with separators, panels appearing below the triggers
- Vertical: steps laid out top to bottom with separators, panels to the right

Actually for horizontal orientation, a typical stepper has:
- A row of step indicators with separators between them
- Then below it, the content for the current step

For a simpler approach where the panel is inside `StepperItem`, horizontal might show each panel hidden/shown based on the active step, while vertical would display them as separate panels below each trigger.

Let me think about this from a usage perspective.
